name: Chocolate Meringue Cake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees.
2. Cover two cookie sheets with baking parchment paper or aluminum foil and draw two 9-inch circles on the paper.
3. Whisk egg whites and lemon juice in a bowl until they hold soft peaks. Add sugar, one spoonful at a time, beating constantly until all sugar is incorporated and meringue is stiff and shiny.
4. Divide meringue between the two circles on parchment; spread evenly to the edges.
5. Bake for 4 hours, then turn off oven. Leave meringues in the oven until cold.
6. Meringues may be made ahead of time and stored in a cardboard box.
7. Chop chocolate into small chunks.
8. Heat 1 cup cream and coffee granules to boiling; drop in chocolate. Remove from heat and stir until chocolate is completely melted and mixture is smooth. Allow to cool, then chill for 1-2 hours.
9. Beat the chocolate mixture until it stiffens and becomes light in color, with a spreading consistency.
10. Spread cream on one meringue disk and sandwich the two meringues together.
11. To freeze disks, put them in freezer until frozen, then wrap in foil.
12. To make sauce: defrost raspberries, puree in blender, strain into a freezer container, stir in 1/4 cup powdered sugar and remaining lemon juice until smooth. Freeze until ready to serve.
13. To serve: defrost cake overnight in refrigerator, unwrap, and set on serving dish. Whip remaining cream with remaining powdered sugar until stiff. Decorate top with cream and finish with candy beans. Pour a puddle of sauce on each plate and set a wedge of cake on top.
